title: Off-chain JWT session token after on-chain handshake verification

artifacts.description: TS class that accepts a verified on-chain handshake result and issues a signed JWT (HS256 or ES256) encoding nullifierHash, scopeCommitment, bolyra_session_nonce, permissions bitmask, and exp. Exposes issue() and verify() methods.
TypeScript interfaces: BolyraSessionClaims (JWT payload shape), SessionTokenConfig (algorithm, secret/key, ttlSeconds), VerifiedHandshakeResult (input from verifyHandshake()).
Re-export SessionTokenIssuer and BolyraSessionClaims from the public SDK surface.
Python verify_session_token() helper. Validates JWT signature (HS256/ES256), checks exp, extracts BolyraSessionClaims as a dataclass. Does not issue tokens — issuance is TS-only.
Python dataclass BolyraSessionClaims mirroring the TS interface: nullifier_hash, scope_commitment, bolyra_session_nonce, permissions, exp, iat.
Specification for the Bolyra session token JWT profile: required claims, algorithm constraints, nonce binding semantics, expiry window recommendations, and replay-prevention model.
Mocha/Chai tests: issue-then-verify round-trip (HS256 and ES256), expired token rejection, tampered claim rejection, mismatched nonce rejection, permissions claim fidelity.
pytest tests for verify_session_token(): valid token acceptance, expired rejection, bad signature rejection, missing required claim rejection.

Developer guide: why session tokens exist (gas cost per tool call), integration pattern for LangChain/LangGraph agent loops, token lifecycle diagram, security considerations (short TTL, nonce binding, no refresh tokens).
Update existing quickstart with a session-token section showing the 3-step flow: verifyHandshake() → issuer.issue() → verify_session_token() per tool call.

steps: Define BolyraSessionClaims interface and SessionTokenConfig in sdk/src/session/types.ts; align claim names with existing verifyHandshake() output fields (nullifierHash, scopeCommitment, sessionNonce).
Add jsonwebtoken (or jose for ESM compatibility) as a dependency in sdk/package.json; audit for CVEs before pinning.

Implement SessionTokenIssuer.issue(handshakeResult, config): validate input, build JWT payload with required claims (nullifier_hash, scope_commitment, bolyra_session_nonce, permissions, iat, exp), sign with HS256 or ES256 based on config.
Implement SessionTokenIssuer.verify(token, config): decode and verify signature, check exp, assert bolyra_session_nonce claim is present, return typed BolyraSessionClaims.
Re-export SessionTokenIssuer and types from sdk/src/index.ts; run tsc --noEmit to confirm no type regressions.
Write Mocha tests covering round-trip, expiry, tampering, nonce mismatch, and both HS256/ES256 algorithm paths; all tests must pass under npm run test:circuits:fast (no on-chain dependency).
Implement Python BolyraSessionClaims dataclass in sdk-python/bolyra/types.py.
Implement verify_session_token(token, secret_or_pubkey, algorithms) in sdk-python/bolyra/session.py using PyJWT; return BolyraSessionClaims or raise BolyraSessionError.
Write pytest suite for the Python helper covering the same failure modes as the TS tests.

Author spec/session-token-profile.md documenting required claims, forbidden claims (no sub, no aud ambiguity), algorithm allow-list (HS256, ES256 only — RS256 explicitly excluded), recommended TTL (300s–900s), and nonce binding semantics.

Update docs/session-tokens.md with LangChain integration pattern: one verifyHandshake per session, issue token, pass token in tool call headers, middleware calls verify_session_token() before executing tool.
Update sdk/QUICKSTART.md with the 3-step session-token section.
Run full test suite (npm test + pytest -v) and confirm zero regressions before marking complete.
dependencies: verifyHandshake() — existing SDK function whose output is the input to SessionTokenIssuer.issue()
BolyraHandshakeResult type — must expose nullifierHash, scopeCommitment, sessionNonce, permissions as typed fields
On-chain verifyMutualHandshake contract — session tokens are only valid downstream of a successful on-chain verification; this implementation does not replace that step
